turns.svg:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <rect width="512" height="512" fill="#8cc152" />
  <ellipse cx="260" cy="360" rx="220" ry="100" fill="#67a53a" />
  <ellipse cx="400" cy="360" rx="80" ry="40" fill="#4c9a2a" />

  <!-- Hole and flag -->
  <circle cx="400" cy="360" r="18" fill="#071019" />
  <circle cx="400" cy="360" r="30" fill="rgba(0,0,0,0.08)" />
  <rect x="396.5" y="240" width="3" height="120" fill="#6b4b2d" />
  <polygon points="399.5,250 399.5,286 430,268" fill="#e63946" />

  <!-- Golf ball on a tee -->
  <circle cx="220" cy="320" r="14" fill="#ffffff" stroke="#d8d8d8" stroke-width="1" />
  <g fill="#cfcfcf">
    <circle cx="214" cy="316" r="2" />
    <circle cx="228" cy="316" r="2" />
    <circle cx="220" cy="308" r="1.6" />
    <circle cx="220" cy="324" r="1.6" />
    <circle cx="212" cy="322" r="1.5" />
    <circle cx="228" cy="322" r="1.5" />
  </g>
  <polygon points="218,336 222,336 220,348" fill="#ff6b6b" />

  <!-- Golf club (shaft and head) -->
  <rect x="120" y="180" width="8" height="210" rx="4" transform="rotate(25 124 185)" fill="#2f2f2f" />
  <path d="M245 336c-18 -4 -34 -10 -42 -16 -6 -4 -10 -12 -7 -18 3 -6 11 -9 17 -12 12 -5 28 -8 42 -12 6 -2 13 1 17 6 6 8 9 18 5 27 -5 12 -18 24 -32 25z" fill="#3a3a3a" transform="translate(-20,-6) rotate(-20 220 320)" />

  <!-- subtle shadows -->
  <ellipse cx="220" cy="338" rx="20" ry="6" fill="rgba(0,0,0,0.12)" />
  <ellipse cx="360" cy="392" rx="40" ry="10" fill="rgba(0,0,0,0.08)" />
</svg>

messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

message.reasoning:
**Preparing shapes for design**

I'm working on a hole design with a location at (400,360) and a radius of 18. The inner circle will be black, while the outer ring will be a darker green. There's a flag at a slight incline, placed vertically, positioned at (400,300). I need to create a club design too, with a shaft extending from the top-left to the ball, using a thick brown or dark grey stroke. I'll also indicate a dimple pattern with small grey circles and ensure no text is included. Now, I'm preparing the JSON string with proper escaping and multiline formatting for coordinates.**Creating SVG for golf scene**

I'm working on SVG content for a golf scene. I need a 512x512 canvas with a background rectangle filled with #8cc152. There’s a ground shadow using an ellipse and a putting green patch. I’ll add a hole and a flagstick, along with a golf ball that includes dimples. The club shaft and head will also be represented. It’s essential to get the dimensions and colors right to maintain clarity and visual appeal. I'll finalize the SVG with proper formatting.**Finalizing SVG JSON string**

I need to ensure that my SVG content is formatted correctly, paying attention to decimal separators and avoiding quotes inside the string. The JSON must have escaped double quotes, so I’ll structure it with a single field labeled "svg." I should also verify that the transform attribute on the club head includes escaped quotes properly. I'll use \n for newlines in the final assembly. It's crucial to be careful with escaping quotes, but backslashes aren't needed, so I’ll focus on getting it right. Now, let’s produce the final JSON string!

I'll use \n for newlines in the final assembly. It's crucial to be careful with escaping quotes, but backslashes aren't needed, so I’ll focus on getting it right. Now, let’s produce the final JSON string!

messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }
